method: Value of Information (VOI) on Unlabeled Samples
title: Value of Information (VOI) on Unlabeled Samples
text_0: **Idea:** VOI treats querying as a decision problem and picks the sample with the highest expected information gain minus its cost, using a one-step lookahead over possible outcomes.

method: Value of Information (VOI) on Labeled Samples
title: Value of Information (VOI) on Labeled Samples
text_0: **Idea:** VOI treats querying as a decision problem and picks the sample with the highest expected information gain minus its cost, using a one-step lookahead over possible outcomes.

title: Value of Information (VOI)
text_0: **Idea:** VOI treats querying as a decision problem and picks the sample with the highest expected information gain minus its cost, using a one-step lookahead over possible outcomes.

method: Monte-Carlo Expected Error Reduction (EER) with Log-Loss
title: Monte-Carlo Expected Error Reduction (EER) with Log-Loss
text_0: EER selects the unlabeled sample whose label, in expectation, would most reduce future log-loss after retraining. In practice it approximates this one-step lookahead with Monte Carlo over possible labels, but it is computationally heavy and sensitive to calibration of class probabilities.

method: Monte-Carlo Expected Error Reduction (EER) with Misclassification-Loss
title: Monte-Carlo Expected Error Reduction (EER) with Misclassification-Loss
text_0: EER selects the unlabeled sample whose label, in expectation, would most reduce future misclassification rate after retraining. In practice it approximates this one-step lookahead with Monte Carlo over possible labels, but it is computationally heavy and sensitive to calibration of class probabilities.
